finding_results.notes: Revised all three cited Megan lines. Line 111: replaced abstract 'meaningful'/'complicated' with concrete toast content ('the custody, about the marriage') and grounded acknowledgment ('We both heard those parts'). Line 115: replaced categorical therapeutic summary ('inappropriate, embarrassing, honest in a way...') with concrete facts and consequences ('drunk at our daughter's wedding,' 'said things about the marriage in front of everyone,' 'Brendan's mother asked me afterward if you were all right') and a more personal acknowledgment that shows strain ('things I'd never heard you say sober. Not to me. Not in eighteen years.'). Line 230: replaced generic reassurance ('It's all right to not have it figured out yet') with a specific observation grounded in this dinner's emotional climax and their shared marital history ('You did that thing with the lamb. You found the one thing you were sure about and you said that instead. You did that our whole marriage.'). All three revised lines maintain Megan's composed, sequential, structurally minded register while anchoring to this dinner, this wedding, and this family's specific circumstances. At least one clause in each shows strain or selection rather than arriving pre-smoothed. Narrator commentary at lines 115 and 232 preserved without modification as it already fits the revised delivery. Contraction level consistent with Megan's 'moderate' profile. No elliptical clipping introduced. Doug's adjacent responses ('It was our daughter's wedding' at 113; 'Yeah, I know' at 234) flow naturally from the revised lines.

pass_summaries.summary: Revised chapter 8's hinge confession and compressed the late chapter so Brendan's admission lands once, Doug deflects through the lamb, and the leftovers/home image carries the aftermath without redundant thematic restatement.

Single finding (ch08_dialogue_002) addressed by revising three Megan dialogue lines from abstract therapeutic summary toward scene-grounded, specific speech. The dinner-table lines now name concrete toast content (the custody, the marriage) and concrete aftermath (Brendan's mother, eighteen years of unspoken truths) rather than packaging the toast into 'meaningful' and 'complicated.' The doorway line now observes Doug's specific behavioral pattern (the lamb deflection) and anchors it in their shared marital history rather than offering generic career reassurance. All revisions preserve Megan's composed, fair, sequential register while introducing friction through pauses, physical gestures, and personal specificity. Narrator commentary and adjacent character responses preserved intact.
